uses: Tragacantha chordorrhiza, also known as the Devil's Claw, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is often used to treat arthritis, back pain, and other musculoskeletal issues. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antioxidant and antispasmodic effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and other conditions. Some studies suggest that Tragacantha chordorrhiza may also have immunomodulatory effects, making it a potential treatment for autoimmune diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.
